content.problemStatement:
You are standing in a queue with NNN people in front of you, numbered 111 to NNN. Person 111 is at the front of the queue, and person NNN is towards the back of the queue, and you are behind person NNN. You have to wait for the people in front of you to exit the queue before you can reach the front, which is your goal.

You know that the iii-th person will take exactly AiA_iAi‚Äč units of time to exit the queue after he gets to the front of the queue.

Further, if you are currently behind person XXX, you can choose to bribe him for BXB_XBX‚Äč rupees, and he will let you swap positions with him. You can choose to swap multiple times, but each time only with the person directly in front of you. Assume that these bribings and swappings can happen instantly.

Since time is money, each unit of time is worth exactly 111 rupee to you. You want to reach the front of the queue for as little cost as possible.

Formally, let TTT denote the time you waited for, and MMM denote the money you spent to reach the front of the queue. You want to minimize (T+M)(T + M)(T+M) and print the minimum value.

Further, there are now QQQ updates to the arrays.


Given 333 integers, i,ai, ai,a and bbb; update Ai=a,Bi=bA_i = a, B_i = bAi‚Äč=a,Bi‚Äč=b.
You should print Q+1Q + 1Q+1 integers, the answer to the problem for the original arrays, and the answers after each update. Note that the updates to the arrays are permanent.


Input Format

The first line contains 222 integers - NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
The next QQQ lines each contain 333 integers - iii, aaa and bbb.


Output Format
Output Q+1Q + 1Q+1 integers, the answer before any update, and the answers after the iii-th update for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§Q1 \le i \le Q1‚Č§i‚Č§Q.
constraints: **Subtask 6 (21 points):** At all times (even after updates), A1‚Č§A2‚Č§‚Ä¶‚Č§ANA_1 \le A_2 \le \ldots \le A_NA1‚Äč‚Č§A2‚Äč‚Č§‚Ä¶‚Č§AN‚Äč and B1‚Č•B2‚Č•‚Ä¶‚Č•BNB_1 \ge B_2 \ge \ldots \ge B_NB1‚Äč‚Č•B2‚Äč‚Č•‚Ä¶‚Č•BN‚Äč holds.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
You can choose to do either of the following:

Wait for person 111. This takes time T=5T = 5T=5 and money M=0M = 0M=0.
Bribe person 111, instantly going to the front of the queue. This takes time T=0T = 0T=0 and money M=4M = 4M=4.

It can be seen that the minimum value of (T+M)(T + M)(T+M) is 444 which is achieved in the second strategy.

Explanation:
The optimal strategy is to bribe the second and the third persons, and wait for the first. This takes time T=2T = 2T=2 and money M=3+2=5M = 3 + 2 = 5M=3+2=5. Thus, the value of (T+M)(T + M)(T+M) is 777.
